title: Shopify AI Store Builder needed for team
description:
We are an eCommerce company looking for a talented and efficient Shopify Store Builder & Operator who can build high-quality, branded Shopify stores using AI tools — with limited resources and fast turnaround times.



This is not a basic theme install job. We need someone who can take a product, a brand name, and minimal assets — and build a fully branded, conversion-optimized Shopify store from scratch using AI for copywriting, product images, and store content. You will be expected to operate at a high level across every part of the store build.



---



?? REQUIRED — REVIEW THIS EXAMPLE BEFORE APPLYING ??



Study this store carefully. This is the standard of quality we expect you to deliver:



----------



Pay attention to the product page structure, AI-generated product images, infographic-style PDP images, benefit sections, customer testimonial layouts, comparison tables, results timelines, FAQ sections, trust badges, and overall branding. If you cannot build a store at this level, please do not apply.



---



THE ROLE



You are an overall Shopify store operator and builder. You will be responsible for the entire store — from theme setup and customization to product images, copywriting, app integrations, and final QA. You must have a deep understanding of Shopify and how to build stores that look professional and convert.



---



AI TOOLS WE USE — YOU MUST BE COMFORTABLE WITH THESE



- Nano Banana Pro — for generating product images, lifestyle shots, infographic visuals, and branded PDP imagery when we have limited or no photography. You will be creating professional product images from scratch using AI.

- Claude Opus 4.6 — for writing all store copy including product descriptions, headlines, benefit sections, FAQ content, comparison copy, and homepage content. You must know how to prompt Claude effectively to get high-converting, brand-appropriate copy.

- You should have a deep understanding of how to use AI as your primary tool for building stores with limited resources. We are not handing you a full brand kit with professional photography — you are building the brand visually and verbally using AI.



---



REQUIREMENTS



- Proven experience building Shopify stores from scratch or redesigning existing ones — with examples

- Deep expertise with Shrine Pro theme specifically — this is the theme we use across our stores

- Ability to generate professional, branded product images using Nano Banana Pro or similar AI image tools when no photography exists

- Strong copywriting skills OR the ability to use Claude Opus 4.6 effectively to produce compelling product descriptions, headlines, benefit copy, FAQs, and full page content

- Knowledge of Shopify apps and integrations — you must be able to set up and configure apps such as Loox (reviews), ---------- , upsell apps, subscription apps,
 marketing integrations, and other conversion-focused tools

- Understanding of conversion-optimized store layouts — including trust badges, urgency elements, social proof sections, comparison tables, ingredient/benefit breakdowns, and results timelines

- Ability to build stores that look and feel like the Elare example above — branded, professional, and high-converting even with limited starting assets

- Must be able to deliver projects quickly without compromising quality

- Fluent in English, both written and spoken

- Strong attention to detail and ability to meet deadlines



---



YOUR TASKS AS A SHOPIFY STORE BUILDER & OPERATOR



- Build complete Shopify stores from scratch using Shrine Pro theme

- Customize theme sections, layouts, and styling to match each brand

- Generate all product images using Nano Banana Pro — including main product shots, lifestyle images, infographic PDP images, and benefit visuals

- Write or generate all store copy using Claude Opus 4.6 — product pages, homepage, about page, FAQ, and any additional content sections

- Set up navigation, collections, product pages, and variant structures

- Install and configure Shopify apps — Loox, ---------- , upsell/cross-sell apps, subscription tools,
 capture, and other functionality-enhancing apps

- Build out trust and conversion elements — money-back guarantee sections, comparison tables, ingredient breakdowns, testimonial carousels, and results timelines

- Ensure the store is fully functional, mobile-optimized, and ready to launch

- Handle revisions and feedback to refine design and content



---



QUESTIONS TO ANSWER



1. How many Shopify stores have you built? Please share live examples or screenshots.

2. Are you experienced with Shrine Pro theme specifically? If so, show examples.

3. Have you used Nano Banana Pro or similar AI image generation tools to create product images for Shopify stores? Please share examples of AI-generated product imagery you've created.

4. Have you used Claude (or similar AI tools) for writing store copy — product descriptions, benefit sections, FAQs, etc.? Describe how you use it in your workflow.

5. Walk us through your process for building a branded store when you're given only a product, a brand name, and minimal assets. How do you go from zero to a finished store?

6. Which Shopify apps have you worked with? Are you comfortable setting up Loox, ---------- , subscription apps, upsell tools, and similar integrations?

7. How do you ensure fast turnaround times without compromising on quality?

8. Can you look at the Elare store example linked above and confidently say you can build at that level? Be honest.

9. Are you comfortable handling revisions and feedback to improve store design and content?



---



COMPENSATION



$50 CAD per store build



This is a part-time position, but we are looking for someone available for long-term collaboration with ongoing projects. We are scaling quickly and will have consistent store builds coming through.



---



*** HOW TO APPLY ***



*You must include your favorite Shopify theme in the subject line of your application for it to be considered (this shows you actually read it and is very important).*



1.) Send us a message answering all 9 questions

2.) Include examples of Shopify stores you've built — specifically stores where you used AI tools for product images and/or copywriting

3.) Share any AI-generated product images you've created using Nano Banana Pro or similar tools

4.) Provide your contact information (WhatsApp, etc.)



---



If you are a strong Shopify store builder who understands how to use AI tools like Nano Banana Pro and Claude Opus 4.6 to build branded, conversion-optimized stores with limited resources — and you can deliver at the quality level shown in our Elare example — we want to hear from you.



If you cannot build at that standard, this is not the right fit. We need someone who can take minimal inputs and deliver a polished, launch-ready store.

title: Process Improvement & Quality Specialist (Coordinator / Specialist Level - Virtual)
description:
Position Overview



We are seeking a Process Improvement & Quality Specialist to support discrete, project based work focused on documenting, standardizing, and improving organizational processes. This is a hands?on, execution focused role suited to an experienced virtual specialist who can independently take assigned process improvement or quality tasks from start to finish.



The Specialist will be responsible for producing clear, usable, and audit ready outputs, including process maps, SOPs, quality documentation, and improvement artifacts. This role does not involve people management or strategy development. Success is measured by high quality deliverables and timely completion.



Key Responsibilities:



- Process maps, workflows, swimlane diagrams, and decision trees

- Create, update, and manage Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs)

- Support CQI initiatives

- Assist with QA activities

- Support process-level performance indicators (KPIs)



Required Skills and Experience



- Experience in process improvement, quality, or operations support

- Proven ability on CQI, QA, lean, process mapping and SOP Development

- Strong written documentation skills

- Attention to detail, clarity and usability

- Experience working remotely as a virtual assistant or specialist

- Ability to manage multiple deliverables 

- Evidence of measurable improvement outcomes



As part of our screening process, candidates are required to submit a short Loom video (maximum 5 minutes). This allows us to better understand your experience, communication style, and ability to deliver value in a virtual environment.



Please Cover the Following in Your Video:



1. Your Background in Process Improvement & Quality - briefly describe your experience related to process mapping and documentation, SOPs, CQI, audit and quality assurance support.



2. How You Deliver Value in a Virtual Role – briefly explain how you approach unclear or messy processes, translate real?world operations into clear and usable documentation, and manage priorities, communication, and accountability in a fully remote environment.



3. CQI, QA, and Lean in Practice – describe one concrete example where you identified a process or quality issue, applied CQI, Lean, or QA tools (such as PDSA, root cause analysis, or waste reduction), and achieved a measurable or meaningful improvement.



4. Tools You Use to Demonstrate Excellence – outline the tools you regularly use for process mapping, SOP creation and version control, quality or audit support, performance or KPI tracking, and virtual collaboration, including how you have used each tool to deliver results.



5. Availability & Remote Setup – confirm your current availability (full?time or part?time), time zone and preferred working hours, and that you have a reliable remote work setup (internet, workspace, and headset).



How to Submit 



- Please include a shareable Loom link with your application or reply. 

- Applicants with no introduction video will not be considered or advanced in the screening process.

title: Business Intelligence & Systems Specialist (Virtual Assistant)
description:
Position Overview



We are seeking a Business Intelligence & Systems Specialist to support organizational reporting, system coordination, and staff technology enablement. This is a hands on, execution focused role suited to a virtual specialist who can independently manage reporting, dashboards, and system support tasks from start to finish.



The Specialist will be responsible for turning data into clear, usable insights, maintaining reliable reporting, and supporting staff with day?to?day system and IT needs across core platforms. This role does not own strategy, budgets, or infrastructure. Success is measured by accurate reporting, dependable systems support, and timely delivery.



Key Responsibilities



- Maintain and update KPI, performance, and operational dashboards

- Prepare routine management and Board ready reports

- Ensure consistency in data definitions, metrics, and reporting outputs

- Respond to adhoc reporting and data requests from leadership

- Support reporting and data coordination across core systems (AlayaCare, VIA, Microsoft 365)

- Document data sources, reports, and system workflows

- Identify data quality or system integration issues and support resolution

- Manage and triage staff IT and system support tickets

- Track trends, repeat issues, and resolution timelines

- Produce simple summaries to support continuous improvement

- Support staff understanding of dashboards and reports through guides and walkthroughs



Required Skills and Experience



- Experience as a Virtual Assistant, Business Intelligence Analyst (junior/intermediate), or Systems Support Specialist

- Strong comfort working with dashboards, spreadsheets, and reporting tools

- Proficiency with Microsoft 365 (SharePoint, Teams, Excel; Power BI an asset)

- Strong documentation, organization, and follow?through skills

- Ability to work independently in a fully virtual environment

- Ability to manage multiple requests and competing priorities

- Experience in healthcare, nonprofit, or service?based organizations is an asset



Screening Requirement – Loom Video Submission



As part of our screening process, candidates are required to submit a short Loom video (maximum 5 minutes). This allows us to better understand your experience, communication style, and ability to deliver value in a virtual environment.



Please Cover the Following in Your Video



1. Your Background in BI, Reporting, and Systems - briefly describe your experience as a virtual assistant, BI or systems support professional, including the types of reports, dashboards, or system support you have handled and the kinds of organizations or teams you have supported.



2. How You Deliver Value in a BI & Systems Role - explain how you help leaders or teams turn data into useful insight, how you ensure accuracy, consistency, and clarity in reporting, and share one example where your work improved decision?making, efficiency, or system usage.



3. Tools, Systems, and Platforms You Have Used - list only the tools you have actually used for reporting, systems, or support, and briefly explain how you used each to deliver results (e.g., Power BI, Microsoft 365, SaaS platforms, ticketing systems, or automation tools).



4. Problem?Solving & Systems Thinking - share one short example of a reporting, data, or system issue you identified, the steps you took to resolve or improve it, and the outcome achieved.



5. Availability & Remote Work Setup - confirm your current availability (hours per week), time zone, preferred working hours, and that you have a reliable internet connection and dedicated remote work setup.



How to Submit



- Please include a shareable Loom link with your application or reply.

- Applicants without an introduction video will not be considered or advanced in the screening process.

title: Operations Assistant - Affordable Housing Projects
description:
We're Leverage Live Local, the #1 affordable housing tax exemption service provider in Florida. The data you touch will support millions of dollars in annual tax savings for property owners while keeping affordable housing projects financially viable.



Our work is only as good as our data. Every error costs clients money and costs us credibility. If you're the kind of person who can't stand messy spreadsheets, keep reading.



ROLE OVERVIEW

We're hiring a detail-obsessed Operations Assistant to own data quality across our growing portfolio of 70+ properties.



You will be responsible for:

- Reviewing spreadsheets and property data for accuracy and completeness

- Organizing rent rolls and tenant records in Excel / Google Sheets

- Identifying missing, inconsistent, or incorrect data and flagging or fixing it

- Cleaning, formatting, and standardizing data for reports and analysis

- Cross-checking information across multiple files to maintain consistency

- Maintaining organized tracking sheets for ongoing compliance projects

- Supporting admin tasks (drafting clear
s, organizing documents, light project coordination)



Success in this role means: clean, reliable data delivered on time, every time.



YOU'RE A GREAT FIT IF:

- Excel/Sheets is second nature (VLOOKUP / INDEX-MATCH, pivot tables, filters, conditional formatting)

- You've done real data cleaning / reconciliation work, not just simple copy-paste

- You naturally spot errors and patterns others miss

- Your written and spoken English are strong and clear

- You can follow structured processes and SOPs without constant supervision

- You already use AI tools (ChatGPT, etc.) to work faster and smarter

- You're comfortable working with confidential financial and tenant data

- You're available full-time during US Central or Eastern Time hours (Mon-Fri)



Bonus (not required):

- Experience with real estate, property management, or affordable housing data

- Experience working long-term with US-based clients



WHY JOIN LEVERAGE LIVE LOCAL:

- Fully remote, full-time position with a stable, fast-growing US company

- Meaningful mission: we help keep affordable housing projects financially viable

- Direct impact: the data you manage supports millions in property tax savings each year

- Clear structure, documented processes, and room to grow into more responsibility



Compensation: $8-$12/hour USD, full time (40 hours/week), depending on experience and skills.



HOW TO APPLY (READ CAREFULLY)

We only review applications that follow all instructions below. This is intentional - it's the first test of your attention to detail.



In your application message, answer these 3 questions in order:

1. Describe your Excel experience in 4-6 sentences. Be specific about functions and features you actually use.

2. Describe a time you found an error in data that others missed. What was the impact?

3. What is your internet speed and computer specs? Include a link to a recent speedtest.net result.



Also attach or link: your CV/resume (PDF preferred) and one sample spreadsheet you created or cleaned (hide any sensitive info).



Applications that do not follow these steps will not be reviewed.



This role is for someone who takes pride in getting things right. If you're meticulous, fast, and serious about your work - we'd love to hear from you.

title: Office Assistant with Microsoft Excel MASTERY
description:
Established in 1984, PTFS, LLC is a commercial services company headquartered in Houston TX. Some of our clients have been longstanding customers of ours for over 40 years. We are extremely data driven and we use Excel heavily for most of our data metrics. We also use Quickbooks Online Advanced for Accounting among others. We utilize the spreadsheet sync function in QBO advanced, and we rely on many of the reports both standard & customized in QBO advanced to make informed decisions. 



We are in process of building a fully remote team in the PH which will eventually take over all US based operations. We are looking for tea
mbers LONG TERM. We value loyalty and tenure-ship and most importantly, availability. We would like all of the tea
mbers we bring onboard to retire with us. We are firm believers that everybody working here deserves the opportunity to grow. Everyone will have a clear understanding of the trajectory of their career path with us. After 1 year, every tea
mber will go through the process of performance review determining promotion / increase in compensation. The metrics we use are:



- Improved understanding of company processes 

- Thoroughness & speed in taking proper actions 

- Frequency of errors / how individual has corrected them

- New responsibilities assumed since hire

- Dependency & Availability

- Does the individual go above & beyond? 

- Is the individual eager to take the lead in any situation?

- Does the individual possess other skills helpful to the company? 



Our line of work is very fast paced with ever-changing requests from clients. Schedules are set but can change at any given 
nt. It is important for tea
mbers to understand what needs to be done when a sudden change is requested by a customer as there are field workers who are dependent on the office to communicate such changes.  As a tea
mber, we need you to be versatile, stepping up or filling in at any given 
nt if another tea
mber is busy or unavailable. Thus, we don't have a separation of duties. Each tea
mber must be able to do EVERYTHING



If you are interested in applying for this position, please answer the following questions and include your response to your message.



On a scale of 1-10 with 10 being complete mastery and 1 being complete novice, what would you say your proficiency level is with the following:



MS Excel (Overall)

Xlookup & Vlookup

Index & Match

Conditional Formatting

Pivot Tables

Power Query

Data Validation

Macros

Dashboards

Advanced Charting



Please list all other programs, apps, software, technical skills that you have knowledge with followed by - Scale 1-10 proficiency level for each



What is your proficiency in the English language? Scale 1-10

Spoken English

Written English

title: Revit Architect — US Architecture Firm
description:
Revit Architect/Engineer — US Architecture Firm (Include the word ELEVATION in your subject line)



We are a US-based architecture firm in Santa Monica, California producing residential and commercial construction documents. We need skilled Revit architects to join our growing remote production team.



Pay is based entirely on what you can do. If you draft floor plans in Revit, that is one rate. If you can run a full set of construction documents from SD through CD, coordinate with engineers, respond to city comments,
 clients, and manage junior staff — that is a very different rate. Show us what you bring.



What You'll Do (Depending on Your Level)

All candidates must be able to:

- Produce construction documents in Revit — plans, elevations, sections, details, schedules

- Work in US Imperial units (feet and inches, not metric)

- Follow US building standards, title block formats, and sheet organization

- Model accurately and keep files clean and organized

- Meet deadlines and communicate clearly in English

- Have a degree and license in Architecture, Civil or MEP engineering



Mid-level candidates should also be able to:

- Produce a full set of CDs with minimal supervision

- Set up Revit templates, families, and project standards

- Coordinate with structural and MEP engineers

- Redline response and plan check comment resolution

- Research building codes and zoning requirements as needed



Senior candidates should also be able to:

- Manage projects from schematic design through permit

-
 and coordinate directly with clients, engineers, and city plan checkers

- Review and QC work from junior tea
mbers

- Structural and/or MEP Revit coordination (not just architectural)

- Manage project schedules and flag delays proactively

- Mentor and guide junior architects on standards and workflow



What We're Looking For

- Strong Revit skills — this is non-negotiable, Revit is our primary tool

- US Imperial units experience — if you have only worked in metric, this is not the right fit

- Experience with US-based architecture firms is a major plus

- MEP and/or Structural Revit coordination experience earns higher pay

- Client communication and project management experience earns higher pay

- Detail-oriented, reliable, and self-directed

- Good written English — you may be
ing clients and city departments directly



Work Details

- Full-time, remote, work from home

- Monday – Saturday, 48 hours per week

- Must have overlap with US Pacific Time for coordination

- Pay range: $1.50 – $5.00/hr USD depending on experience and capabilities

- Payment via Wise (USD, monthly invoicing)

- Time tracking via Hubstaff is required — this is non-negotiable

- Communication via Google Chat (primary) and 
 (screen sharing during all working hours)



Pay Tiers (To Be Transparent)

- $1.50 – $2.00/hr: Junior — Revit drafting and modeling under supervision

- $2.00 – $3.50/hr: Mid-level — Full CD sets, engineer coordination, comment response

- $3.50 – $5.00/hr: Senior — Project management, client communication, MEP/Structural, staff management



Your starting rate will be based on the interview. If you grow into more responsibility, your rate grows with you.



To Apply — Read This Carefully

1. Include the word ELEVATION in your
 subject line. Applications without this word will not be reviewed.

Attach your resume.

2. Attach a portfolio or 3–5 samples of your Revit work. Construction documents preferred — not just 3D renders. Show us floor plans, sections, details, schedules. If you have done MEP or Structural coordination, include examples.

3. (IMPORTANT) Record a short Loom video (2–3 minutes, camera ON, no script). Tell us: What is your Revit experience? What types of projects have you worked on? What is your strongest skill? Have you worked with US firms before?

4. Tell us which pay tier you think you fall into and why.

5. Confirm you work in US Imperial units.

6. Confirm you understand and accept time tracking via Hubstaff during all working hours.

7. Confirm you have a Wise account or are willing to set one up.

8. Confirm you are available for live screen sharing on 
 during working hours.



Applications that do not follow these instructions will not be considered. We are looking for people who pay attention to detail — your application is your first test.

title: Medical Assistant – Weight Loss Clinic
description:
We are seeking a highly responsible, detail-oriented, and compassionate Medical Assistant to join our fast-paced weight loss clinic.

Reliability and accountability are non-negotiable.



You will play a key role in managing patient communication, coordinating medications and labs, and supporting the clinical team to ensure a seamless patient experience.



Key Responsibilities



Patient Communication & Support

Manage ongoing communication with patients via phone,
, and text. Provide clear, professional, and empathetic guidance throughout their program.



Medication & Peptide Coordination

Assist with ordering medications and peptides, tracking shipments, and sending accurate dosing instructions to patients (including GLP-1 protocols).



Lab Coordination

Place lab orders, track completion, and ensure results are uploaded and ready for provider review.



Clinical Documentation

Prepare and maintain accurate clinical notes and patient records within our systems.



Follow-Up & Accountability

Ensure no patient, lab, or task falls through the cracks. Proactively follow up and take ownership of outcomes.



Team Collaboration

Work closely with coaching, medical, and admin teams to provide a high-quality, seamless patient experience.



Required Skills & Qualifications



GLP-1 / Weight Loss Experience Preferred

Familiarity with semaglutide, tirzepatide, Ozempic, or similar medications is strongly preferred.



Excellent Communication Skills

Strong verbal and written English. Able to communicate clearly, confidently, and professionally.



Confidence to Speak Up

Comfortable asking questions, raising concerns, and communicating proactively.



Highly Detail-Oriented & Organized

Able to manage multiple workflows without errors.



Tech-Savvy

Comfortable using CRMs (Zoho, GoHighLevel), Google Sheets/Docs, and online systems.



Reliable & Accountable

Consistently follows through and takes ownership of responsibilities.



Fast Learner

Able to quickly adapt in a fast-paced, evolving environment.



Job Details



Type: Full-time

Schedule: Monday to Friday, 7:00 AM – 4:00 PM (MST)

Compensation: $7–$10/hour (based on experience)

Location: Remote (must have stable internet and a quiet workspace)



Why Join Us?



Join a mission-driven team helping patients transform their health through advanced medical weight loss and peptide protocols. This is a hands-on role in a growing clinic with strong opportunities to learn and grow.



How to Apply

title: Senior Klaviyo Email & SMS Specialist — Premium E-Commerce Brand Remote
description:
We run a premium decorative lighting brand on Shopify with a global customer base.
 is a core revenue driver and we need the best Klaviyo operator available — not someone good, the best.

If you are still learning Klaviyo, please do not apply. If you have never directly driven significant revenue through
, please do not apply. We are not a training ground and we will not waste each other's time.



You are who we're looking for if:



- You have 3+ years owning Klaviyo end-to-end (you built the flows yourself, not supervised someone else)

- You can name the exact revenue impact you've had on past brands — percentage of store revenue, recovery rates, CVR lifts

- You live inside the data and produce reports without being asked

- You have handled deliverability issues, not just avoided them

- SMS is part of your toolkit, not something you've "looked into"





The role covers:

Full flow architecture, campaign calendar, segmentation, A/B testing, deliverability, SMS, and weekly revenue reporting. You own the channel. No handholding.



To Apply

Send your application with the subject line: Klaviyo — [Your Name]

Include only these three things:

1. One result. Pick the single best outcome you have driven through Klaviyo. Tell us the brand context, what you built or changed, and the exact numbers before and after.

2. Screenshot or description of your best flow. Show us the structure — triggers, splits, timing, logic. If you can't share a screenshot, describe it in detail. We want to see how you think.

3. Complete this task:



You are given a Klaviyo account for a premium home décor brand on Shopify. The account has 45,000 contacts, a basic welcome series (3
s), an abandoned cart flow (1
), and no other automations.
 currently drives 9% of total store revenue. List open rate is 18?ross all sends.

Write your 90-day action plan. Be specific — name the flows you'd build first and why, how you'd approach segmentation, what you'd fix in deliverability, and what KPIs you'd target by day 30, 60, and 90.

Maximum 300 words. Do not write in generalities. We will use this to rank every applicant.



Applications missing any of these three elements will be deleted without response.

title: Cold Caller — AI Agency (Short Script, Simple Role)
description:
About the role

We run an AI voice and chat agency and need a confident phone caller to reach out to mobile welding businesses on our behalf. This is a simple, low-pressure role — you are NOT selling anything. You are making one quick ask per call and logging the result.



What you'll do

• Call mobile welding businesses from a provided list

• Confirm they are speaking with the business owner

• Confirm the business website on file is correct

• Log the result in our CRM (GoHighLevel) — we'll train you

• Our system automatically sends the demo once you update the record



What you will NOT do

• No hard selling

• No long pitches

• No objection handling — if they're not interested, you move on



The script

You will follow a short, word-for-word script. The entire call is under 30 seconds. We provide full training before you start.



Requirements

• Clear, confident spoken English

• Reliable internet and phone/VOIP setup

• Comfortable making outbound calls

• Basic computer skills to update CRM records

• Previous cold calling experience is a plus but not required



Hours & pay

• Hours are flexible 3-8 hrs New York eastern standard time

• Paid per hour

• Part-time to start with room to grow



To apply

Please send a short voice recording (30–60 seconds) introducing yourself and why you'd be a good fit. Written applications without a voice note will not be considered.

title: Part-Time Personal VA — Daily Organization, Reminders & Life Admin
description:
Part-Time Personal VA — Daily Organization, Reminders & Life Admin (Include the word ANCHOR in your subject line)



I am a business owner based in Los Angeles, California. I run multiple companies and my days are packed. I need a personal virtual assistant to keep my personal life organized so I can focus on work without things falling through the cracks.



This is a part-time role — 4 hours per day, 6 days a week (Monday through Saturday). Your job is to be the person who makes sure I never miss an appointment, forget to follow up on something, or feel overwhelmed by my personal to-do list.

This is a personal assistant role — not a business role. You will manage my personal life only.



What You'll Do

- Start every day by telling me exactly what meetings and appointments I have — clear, simple, no clutter

- Manage my personal calendar: schedule appointments, move things around, prevent conflicts

- Set up reminders and call or text me directly when something is coming up — no
 reminders, I need a phone call or text message

- Keep a running personal to-do list and make sure things actually get done — follow up with me if I forget

- Book doctor appointments, dentist, personal errands — anything that requires scheduling

- Plan trips: research flights, hotels, itineraries, book everything once approved

- Online shopping and errands: research products, compare options, place orders

- Family logistics: medical appointments, family events

- Follow up with people who owe me money — track who owes what, send reminders, keep me updated

- Proactively tell me when my week is getting too full and suggest what to move or cancel

- Generally make sure I am not overwhelmed — you are the person who keeps my personal life running smoothly



What We're Looking For

- Highly organized and proactive — you do not wait to be told, you anticipate what needs to happen

- Reliable and consistent — you show up every day at the same time, no excuses

- Comfortable calling and texting (not just messaging) — you will call me directly for reminders

- Good English communication — written and spoken

- Detail-oriented — nothing slips through the cracks

- Trustworthy and discreet — you will have access to personal information

- Experience as a personal assistant, executive assistant, or similar role preferred

- Tech-savvy: Google Calendar, Google Tasks, basic online research



Work Details

- Part-time: 4 hours per day, 6 days per week (Monday–Saturday)

- Hours: 8:00 AM – 12:00 PM Pacific Time (this is 12:00 AM – 4:00 AM Philippine Time — please only apply if you are comfortable with this schedule)

- Payment via Wise (USD, monthly invoicing)

- Time tracking via Hubstaff is required

- Communication via Google Chat and phone/text for reminders



To Apply — Read This Carefully

1. Include the word ANCHOR in your
 subject line. Applications without this word will not be reviewed.

Attach your resume.

2. (VERY IMPORTANT) Record a short Loom video (1–2 minutes, camera ON, no script). Tell us: Who are you? How do you stay organized? Give an example of a time you helped someone stay on top of their schedule or personal tasks.

3. Confirm you are comfortable working 12:00 AM – 4:00 AM Philippine Time, 6 days a week.

4. Confirm you have a Wise account or are willing to set one up.

5. Confirm you understand and accept time tracking via Hubstaff.



Applications that do not follow these instructions will not be considered.

title: E-commerce Finance & Profit Analyst
description:
E-commerce Finance & Profit Analyst (Full-Time / Long-Term / Remote)



Job Overview



We run an e-commerce business and are looking for a reliable Finance & Profit Analyst to fully take over all financial calculations.



Your job is simple but very important:

All numbers must be correct.

Every week we receive a clear profit sheet.



If you are detail-oriented, strong with spreadsheets, and experienced with e-commerce numbers, this role is for you.







Your Main Responsibilities

• Calculate weekly profits (per store & total)

• Track and calculate all costs, including:

• Product cost (COGS)

• Advertising spend

• Payment processing fees

• Refunds & chargebacks

• Software/tools

• Handle VAT calculations (sales VAT, VAT payable)

• Create and maintain a weekly profit sheet

• Reconcile data from:

• Shopify / WooCommerce

• Stripe / PayPal

• Meta Ads / Google Ads

• Double-check all numbers for accuracy

• Report issues or unusual results immediately





Required Skills & Experience

• Experience with e-commerce finance, bookkeeping, or profit tracking

• Strong knowledge of Google Sheets or Excel

• Experience creating weekly profit reports

• Understanding of VAT / sales tax basics

• Very high attention to detail

• Able to work independently and meet weekly deadlines

• Good written English







Nice to Have (Big Plus)

• Shopify experience

• EU VAT experience

• Experience with ad platforms (Meta, Google, TikTok)

• Automation or dashboard experience





Working Hours & Pay

• Full-time (2 hours/week)

• Long-term position (not a short project)

• Salary: based on experience (please include your expected monthly salary)







How to Apply (IMPORTANT)



To be considered, start your application with the word “PROFIT” so we know you read the full job post.



Then include:

1. Your experience with e-commerce finance

2. Examples of profit sheets or reports you have created

3. Tools you are experienced with (Sheets, Excel, accounting tools)

Applications without the word “PROFIT” will be ignored.

-Clean up sound quality, remove filler words, pauses, background noise, and awkward cuts

-Add intro/outro, transitions, music, and branding elements

-Sync audio and video when needed

-Export final episodes in the correct formats for publishing

-Create simple thumbnails or cover graphics when needed

-Upload or organize edited files in shared folders



Social Media Content Creation



-Repurpose podcast episodes into short-form content for Instagram, TikTok, YouTube Shorts, and

-Pull strong hooks, soundbites, and teachable 
nts from episodes

-Add captions/subtitles to video clips

-Create branded graphics, quote posts, carousels, and promotional assets

-Write engaging captions that match the brand voice

-Help brainstorm content ideas based on podcast topics

-Organize content for scheduling and posting



Qualifications:



-Proven experience editing podcasts and short-form content

-Strong skills in video editing and audio editing

-Proficiency in tools such as CapCut, Adobe Premiere Pro,  Audition, Canva, and or similar platforms

-Strong understanding of social media trends and what makes content engaging

-Ability to identify viral-worthy clips, hooks, and standout 
nts

-Good eye for pacing, storytelling, branding, and visual presentation

-Strong English communication skills

-Organized, responsive, and able to meet deadlines

-Able to follow creative direction while also bringing fresh ideas



Preferred:



-Experience working with personal brands, coaches, speakers, podcasters, or entrepreneurs

-Experience creating reels, carousels, and promotional content from long-form video

-Familiarity with content strategy and audience engagement

-Basic graphic design skills



What Success Looks Like in This Role:



-Podcast episodes are edited cleanly and delivered on time

-Social media clips feel engaging, on-brand, and shareable

-Long-form content is consistently repurposed into high-value short-form assets

-The brand shows up online with consistency, quality, and creativity

Compensation : $200 monthly for 10-15 hours weekly

To Apply:

Please send the following to
: 



-A short introduction about yourself via YouTube unlisted and share the link

-Your experience with podcast editing and social media content creation

-Links to samples of your work or portfolio

-The editing tools/software you use

-Your availability 



Bonus:

Please include the word “PODCAST SUPPORT” in your application subject line so we know you read the full job description.

title: Social Media Manager
description:

Website: 

Company Location: United States

Position: Remote (Philippines-based)

Payment Frequency: Semi-Monthly

Contract Type: Long-term with growth opportunities



What We Offer:

• Direct impact on product architecture and technology decisions

• Continuous learning with access to latest AI and automation technologies

• Performance-based bonuses for exceptional contributors

• Flexible work arrangements

• Professional and marketing development training



Core Requirements (Tasks may vary depending on the project or client requirements):

• The ideal candidate will have hands-on experience working directly for these platforms,

• Possessing deep insights into social media platforms algorithms, audience engagement strategies

• Understand and implement best practices for content creation.

• Strategic thinker with a passion for social media and a knack for storytelling.



Qualifications

• Minimum of 3 years of experience in social media marketing, working with Instagram, TikTok and YouTube.

• Assist in managing social media calendar and hitting content approval and publishing deadlines

• Assist in managing social media projects using our task management tool

• Strong analytical skills with the ability to interpret data and make data-driven decisions.

• Ability to respond to DMs in a conversational tone aligned with Company’s brand voice

• Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with a creative flair for storytelling.

• Familiarity with social media management tools and analytics platforms.

• Ability to work in a fast-paced environment and manage multiple projects simultaneously.

• A passion for social media trends, pop culture, and digital marketing.

• Experience with Canva, Capcut, and Metricool or other graphic design, video editing and content scheduling tools.

• Stay up to date on social media trends and best practices

•

Bonuses Qualifications

• Experience working with Instagram, TikTok, YouTube, other major social media platforms.

• Experience with video editing and content creation tools.

• Knowledge of SEO and its impact on social media performance.

• Previous experience in influencer marketing and partnerships.





Application Tips:

• Make sure your most recent work experience is updated on your resume AND highlight your Social Media experience clearly

• Comment the word "Social" both in 1) the subject of your reply AND also the body of the reply.  If you don't do this, your application will automatically be disqualified.

title: Cold Caller - Sales Representative Real Estate
description:
Part-Time Cold Calling Sales Rep / Real Estate (Land)



3-Month Contract | 20 hrs/week | Opportunity to Extend



We’re not looking for average — we’re looking for someone who thrives on outperforming targets and turning discipline into results.



At our company, we operate by three core values: Relentless Excellence, Disciplined Results, and Continuous Innovation. These aren’t just words — they guide how we work every day.



This role is focused primarily on cold calling landowners, identifying selling opportunities, and qualifying motivated sellers to keep our acquisition pipeline strong. If you’re competitive, organized, and enjoy conversations that lead to deals, this role could be a strong fit.

, or DM about this role

- Contract: 3-month contract with opportunity to extend

- Payment: Weekly (Mondays via 
)

title: Executive & Operations Assistant
description:
Real Estate & Short-Term Rental Business (Remote)

IMPORTANT – READ FIRST

To be considered for this role, you must record a 2-minute selfie video, attach your resume/profile and
 it to:





In your video, please briefly cover:

- A little about yourself

- Your strengths and past experience

- Why you believe you’d be a great fit for this role

Applications without a video will not be reviewed.



About Us:

We operate a fast-growing real estate and short-term rental (Airbnb) management company managing multiple high-end properties across North America. Our business is systems-driven, detail-oriented, and focused on delivering excellent guest and client experiences.



We are looking for a highly organized, reliable, and proactive Virtual Assistant who can help manage administrative tasks, follow-ups, coordination, and outreach — and who takes ownership of their work.



Role Overview

This role is ideal for someone who:

Loves organization, checklists, and structure (can create and implement systems)

Is confident communicating via
, messages and over the phone

Follows up without being asked

Can handle multiple tasks without things slipping through the cracks

You will work closely with the owner and operations team to keep things running smoothly behind the scenes.



Key Responsibilities

Administrative & Operations Support

Track tasks, deadlines, and follow-ups

Maintain organized Google Sheets and documentation

Use AI to create Automations and Streamline Operations

Assist with STR licensing, renewals, and compliance tracking

Coordinate with cleaners, vendors, and internal tea
mbers



Communication & Outreach

Follow up with clients, vendors, municipalities, and partners

Send professional
s and messages

Pre-qualify inquiries and organize information before escalation

Escalate issues clearly and with context (not panic)



Process & Quality Control

title: Junior 3D Artist Trainee (Davao Based / Stay-in)
description:
Build a Lifetime Skill in Davao: Become a "Japan-Quality" Creator from Zero Experience

Architectural 3D Designer (Trainee) Wanted



? Become a "Japan-Quality" Creator from Zero

At MSarchviz, we are building a team from scratch in Davao to reach the high standards of Japanese Architectural Visualization. We are looking for our "Pioneer" members to grow with us.



? To Those Who Want to Become Professional Creators

This industry is known globally as "Architectural Visualization (Archviz)." In Japan, it is a specialized profession called "Architectural Perspective." Our job is to use computers to create realistic images, videos, and VR of buildings before they are built. This is different from being an Architectural Designer or a CAD Operator. If you are unsure of the difference, please search for it online—you will understand immediately.



? What is Required to Become a Professional

While we welcome beginners regardless of educational background, mastering this skill requires the ability to strictly follow "MS Rules" while learning Japanese professional standards, manners, and etiquette. Even if you have experience or a degree, you cannot work at MS if you lack these core abilities.



Specifically, this means: your attitude toward learning, respect for your mentors, time management, and the honesty to apologize without making excuses when you make a mistake—analyzing the cause and improving yourself. You must understand instructions deeply and execute them exactly as told, without making your own assumptions.



Surprisingly, many people cannot do this.

MS clients are major Japanese corporations. They demand high quality, fast response, and zero mistakes. To complete such work, the abilities mentioned above are essential.



Therefore, we prioritize hiring beginners for this position.

Initially, we focus more on developing these professional mindsets than technical skills. Once you master these mindsets, the technical skills will follow naturally. These are skills that will be valuable to you throughout your entire life, even outside of work.



Becoming a professional creator requires an enormous amount of time and effort from both the teacher (me) and the student (you). Thus, if you have a half-hearted attitude like "I'm just a little interested" or "I just want to earn easy money," you will not be able to continue, and I will not be able to fulfill my responsibility to teach you. Apply only if you deeply understand this and have the strong determination to "become a professional creator." For those who take this leap with such resolve, I will dedicate my time and effort to teaching you everything, just like a parent.



? MSarchviz Motto: "Mistakes are a Chance to Grow"

Although I have said many strict things, we do not expect you to be perfect from the start. Everyone fails in the beginning. What matters is that when you make a mistake, you do not blame your environment or others. You must have the honesty to admit your error, apologize, and the tenacity to analyze "why" it happened and implement improvements immediately.



This is how a "Mistake becomes a Chance to Grow." By repeating this process, you will steadily grow into a professional creator.



? Requirements



No specific educational background, experience, or knowledge required (Beginners preferred).



Female, 18–25 years old only (Due to the 3-year training period and safety in the female-only dormitory).



Desire to learn high Japanese quality standards and professional rules.



Strong will to work long-term.



Ability to stay in the dormitory in Davao and focus on work.



Note: There will be an aptitude test during the selection process.



? Selection Process & Allowance



Hiring Test (Approx. 5 hrs): No allowance (Online from home).



Practical Test (40–60 hrs): 500 PHP per day (Dormitory stay required).



Final Training Test (Approx. 3 months): 12,000 PHP per month (Dormitory stay required).



? After Official Hiring



Starting Salary: From 18,000 PHP per month (Training continues while working). Bonus once a year.



Future: 30,000 PHP or more (Expected after approx. 3 years, depending on skill and contribution).



? Working Hours & Commitment



Standard Hours: 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM.



Practice & Overtime: Until you reach professional standards, you will need to practice and work overtime after 6:00 PM, as well as work on Saturdays during busy seasons. You must have the responsibility to complete your tasks as a professional, not just a "9-to-5" mindset.



? How to Apply

This work requires extremely high attention to detail—such as the ability to read and understand this long job posting.



Send the password "READY 2026" at the very beginning of your message.



Submit your Resume with a photo. You MUST include your birth date, age, gender, address, and civil status.



If you don't have a resume, you can create one for free here:



Canva Resume Maker



JobStreet Resume Template



If you skip even one of these steps, you will be disqualified. Your hiring test begins here.



? A Final Message

It is okay if you lack confidence now. It is natural because you have no experience. Once you can do the work, it won't be about "confidence"—it will be "normal" for you. Confidence itself is of no use in this profession.



My educational background is only junior high school. Yet, I have done this work for over 25 years. If you learn honestly from my teaching, you will surely become a professional creator.



Why not take the first step toward a new life with me at MSarchviz?

I sincerely look forward to your challenge.

title: Client Operations Coordinator — US Architecture and Construction Firm
description:
Client Operations Coordinator — US Architecture & Construction Firm (Include the word BLUEPRINT in your subject line)



We are a US-based architecture and construction firm in Santa Monica, California looking for a sharp, reliable Client Operations Coordinator to be the voice and operational backbone of our company. You will be the primary point of contact for clients, subcontractors, and vendors — so a clear, confident American accent and professional phone presence are non-negotiable.



This is a full-time remote role with real responsibility. You will coordinate across two businesses (architecture and construction), manage day-to-day operations, coordinate active construction projects, and keep everything running so the principal can focus on design and business development. This is not a virtual assistant role — this is the person who runs the office.



What You'll Do

Office & Client Operations:

- Answer and make phone calls as the office using a US phone number (Google Voice)

- Communicate with clients: scheduling, updates, issue resolution

- Accounts receivable: track invoices, follow up on payments, call until paid

- Accounts payable: process contractor and vendor invoices, track what's owed, prepare payments for approval

-
 triage: flag urgent items, handle routine communication, keep things moving

- Calendar management: schedule meetings, coordinate availability

- Review team timesheets (Hubstaff), track attendance and hours

- Deliver a daily morning briefing and weekly operations report

- Oversee and coordinate with other tea
mbers (Operations VA, Bookkeeper, Permits VA)



Construction Project Coordination (2–3 Active Jobsites):

- Call and follow up with US subcontractors — get bids, confirm schedules, chase deliverables

- Track project timelines and flag delays before they become problems

- Coordinate between subs, engineers, inspectors, and the principal

- Collect lien waivers from subcontractors before payments are released — chase until you have them all

- Collect vendor documentation: insurance certs, lien waivers, W-9s

- Follow up with building departments on permits, inspections, and plan check status



What We're Looking For:

- Clear, confident American accent — you will be on the phone with US clients and subcontractors daily

- BPO, call center, or client-facing operations background strongly preferred

- Experience as a team leader, supervisor, account manager, or operations coordinator

- Not afraid to pick up the phone and chase people — subcontractors, building departments, clients who owe money

- Highly organized, detail-oriented, and proactive — you anticipate problems before they happen

- Strong written and spoken English

- Comfortable managing multiple priorities across two businesses

- Tech-savvy: Google Workspace, 
, Hubstaff — you pick up new tools fast

- Construction or architecture industry experience is a big plus but not required — what matters is that you can learn fast and aren't intimidated by the industry



Work Details:

- Full-time, remote, work from home

- Must be available during US Pacific Time business hours

- Payment via Wise (USD, monthly invoicing)

- Time tracking via Hubstaff is required — this is non-negotiable

- Communication via Google Chat (primary) and 
 (screen sharing during working hours)



To Apply — Read This Carefully

1. Include the word BLUEPRINT in your
 subject line. Applications without this word will not be reviewed.

Attach your resume.

2. Record a short Loom video (2–3 minutes, camera ON, no script). In the video, tell us: Who are you? Why are you a great fit for this role? Give an example of a time you managed a difficult client or operational situation.

3. Confirm that you understand and accept time tracking via Hubstaff during all working hours.

4. Confirm you have a Wise account or are willing to set one up for payment.

5. Confirm you are available for live screen sharing on 
 during working hours.



Applications that do not follow these instructions will not be considered. We are looking for someone who pays attention to detail — this application is your first test.

title: Catalog & Optimization Lead: Home Improvement & Kitchen (EU)
description:
We are a leading Dutch e-commerce brand in the Home Improvement & Kitchen niche. After dominating the NL market on 
, we expanded into Germany in early 2026. Our brands hold in total over 200+ ASINs, and are looking for a specialist who takes ownership of Visibility and Conversion Optimization.



The Workflow & Trial

You will work independently to hit growth targets. Your goal is to work together with PPC and a designer to make sure that the listings are optimized, and converting, while being able to work by deadlines for priority listings. 



We start with a paid trial period on a specific segment of our ASINS. Success is measured by your ability to improve listing health and conversion. If results are met, you will take over the full catalog.



How to Apply (No Generic AI Templates)

To be considered, start your message with the word "HAMMER" and answer these 5 questions:



Market Entry Logic: When expanding to a new marketplace (e.g., DE), what is your process? Do you rely on Amazon’s automated "Build International Listings" tools, or do you have a manual workflow to ensure market-specific SEO?



The "Why": Why are you looking for a new brand now? Are you currently managing other accounts, or what was the reason for leaving your last long-term partner?



Scale & Results: What is the largest EU account you have personally handled (ASIN count/Revenue)? What specific ranking or CVR results did you achieve in a non-English marketplace?



Team Influence: How do you direct a Designer and a PPC Specialist to ensure an optimization actually converts? What specific data do you give them?



The Proudest Fix: Describe the most complex Flat File or Parent/Child variation error you have personally solved. Walk us through your logic.



Core Responsibilities

Conversion Ownership: Constant A/B testing and optimization of titles, bullets, and backend keywords to drive CVR.



Visibility Tracking: Maintaining a "Before vs. After" log for all changes to prove impact on organic ranking.



New Market Expansion: Leading the technical setup and localization for new EU marketplaces.



Catalog Health: Managing 50+ product families, resolving suppressed listings, and managing FBA/FBM transitions.



Requirements

3+ Years of Amazon Catalog/Flat File mastery.

Proficient in task tools .

Language: Expert English + proven experience using tools for high-quality, native-feel German/Dutch SEO.



What We Offer

We provide the strategy; you provide the execution. We operate on a high-trust, high-autonomy model where we don't micromanage your hours or your methods, we manage by results. If the conversion rates are climbing and the catalog is healthy, you have full authority over your workflow.

A long-term partnership, with incentives and bonuses based on milestones.

High-level strategic support with room for professional autonomy.

Prime Notary is a growing regional and nationwide notary signing service seeking a motivated, detail-oriented Client Intake & Scheduling Specialist to join our operations team. This role is critical to our daily success, client experience, and appointment conversion.



You will serve as the first point of contact for inbound client inquiries, managing calls, texts, and
s while coordinating notary signing appointments from intake through completion. This position requires strong communication skills, attention to detail, and the ability to confidently guide clients toward booking the appropriate services.



This is a stable, long-term opportunity with performance-based earning potential. The stronger your results, the more you can earn. We value consistency, accountability, and professionalism—and we invest in tea
mbers who deliver.





KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:



Manage incoming phone calls, text messages, and
s from clients and notary signing agents



Identify opportunities to convert inbound inquiries into booked appointments by confidently guiding clients toward appropriate notary services

Collaborate with operations managers and internal tea
mbers to ensure client needs are met

Accurately document all client interactions and transactions



Perform follow-up surveys or feedback collection to identify service improvements



Success in this role is measured by responsiveness, appointment conversion, accuracy, and overall client satisfaction.





REQUIREMENTS:



Strong phone presence with excellent English communication skills (required)

title: Part-time Professional and Personal Assistant
description:
I am a founder, executive coach, fractional marketing leader, and author preparing for the national launch of my book Dear Daughter on March 9, 2027. I need a dedicated Professional and Personal Assistant to be my operational right hand, keeping my business and personal life running smoothly so I can stay focused on the work that only I can do.



This role is one half of a two-person support team. A separate Marketing and PR VA handles marketing execution, content publishing, and launch support. Your focus is the operational and personal backbone: my calendar, my inbox, my clients, my family logistics, and the systems that keep everything moving.



Primary Outcome: My business and personal life run smoothly because you manage details proactively, communicate before I have to ask, and never let anything fall through the cracks.





Core Responsibilities



Calendar and Inbox Management

• Manage Kelli's full business and personal calendar daily

• Coordinate meetings, coaching sessions, podcasts, speaking engagements, and discovery calls

• Triage and manage Kelli’s business and personal

• Draft responses in Kelli's voice: warm, direct, never corporate or transactional

• Manage in-person meeting logistics including location selection, reservations, and 30-minute drive time buffers before and after

• Use scheduled send so all outgoing
s arrive between 7:00 AM and 6:00 PM Central Time

• You will be cc'd on
s during the 30-day trial period so Kelli can monitor tone and accuracy



Family Logistics Support

• Monitor school communications and add all dates to the family calendar

• Track daughter’s dance schedule 

• Track son’s tutoring sessions, therapy appointments, and basketball schedule

• Follow up with husband to collect and sync his schedule to the family calendar

• Maintain the shared family calendar 



Client and Business Operations

• Prepare monthly client invoices before the 1st of each month

• Monitor coaching session tracking sheet and flag any client who is falling behind on their contracted sessions

• Build and maintain a prospect tracker in Excel in OneDrive

• Provide weekly structured updates on open items, pending follow-ups, and upcoming deadlines

• Identify inefficiencies in systems or processes and proactively suggest improvements

• Acknowledge all inbound inquiries within 1 business day, even when a full response requires more time



Speaking and Podcast Support (Shared with Marketing VA)

• Research speaking groups, professional organizations, and conference opportunities aligned with Kelli's topics and audience

• Maintain the speaking and podcast opportunity tracker

• Coordinate booking logistics for confirmed engagements: calendar updates, confirmations, and pre-engagement prep

• Hand off outreach drafts and pitch logistics to the Marketing VA for execution



Please submit your application through the online application system. Your application must include all of the following. Applications missing any item will not be reviewed.



Required Application Materials

1 Resume: Your current resume, including relevant experience, tools you have worked with, and any links to work samples or profiles you would like to share.

2 Calendar and Prioritization Simulation (1 to 2 pages): You have been given the following morning: a client calls needing to reschedule a coaching session, a school
 arrives flagging a permission form due tomorrow, Kelli has back-to-back meetings until noon, and a speaking inquiry lands in the inbox. Walk through how you would handle this morning, what you would prioritize, what you would communicate and to whom, and what would not happen until the afternoon.

3 Voice Matching Exercise (1 page): Draft a brief, warm
 on Kelli's behalf responding to an inbound inquiry from a woman interested in executive coaching. Use the Legacy Rising brand voice: warm, direct, relational, never corporate or transactional.

4 Why This Role (1 short paragraph): Tell us in your own words why this specific role appeals to you and what you would bring to it.



Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis. All four items are required.

title: Product Lister Shopify
description:
Are you detail-oriented and love bringing products to life online? We're looking for a Product Lister who thrives on organization, accuracy, and building a strong fashion e-commerce store.



About the Role



As a Product Lister for our Shopify fashion stores, you'll be responsible for building and maintaining our product catalog. Using our proven systems, AI tools, and detailed SOPs, you'll turn supplier data into clear, ready-to-sell product listings that convert visitors into customers.



Key Responsibilities



• List up to 25 products per store daily following our step-by-step SOP

• Use our AI prompts to generate optimized titles and descriptions then refine them

• Accurately input product variants such as sizes, colors, and fits from supplier data

• Upload and format product images according to our brand guidelines

• Set competitive pricing using our margin calculator and pricing rules

• Organize products into collections with proper tags and SEO optimization

• Quality-check every listing for accuracy before publishing

• Track your work and report daily progress in our project management system



What We're Looking For



• Shopify experience. You know the admin panel inside and out

• Laser-sharp attention to detail. One pricing error can cost hundreds

• Process-driven mindset. You like working with SOPs and structured systems

• Self-motivated and reliable. You can work independently without supervision

• Tech-savvy. Comfortable with AI tools, spreadsheets, and new platforms

• English proficiency for light editing of AI-generated product descriptions

• Available 40 hours per week with consistent availability

• Previous fashion e-commerce or product listing experience preferred but not required



What We Offer



• Complete training system. Detailed SOP walks you through everything

• AI-powered workflow. We provide the prompts, you refine the output

• Flexible remote work. Work from anywhere with reliable internet

• Consistent workload. Up to 25 products daily with room to scale

• Clear expectations. Everything is documented step by step

• Growth opportunities. Expand to multiple stores as you prove yourself

• Supportive environment. We help you succeed and improve your workflow



Why You'll Love This Job



No creative guessing. Follow our proven system

AI does most of the work. You refine and structure

Immediate impact. Your listings go live every day

Skill building. Learn Shopify, SEO, and fashion e-commerce operations

Stable income. Consistent daily work instead of short projects



Compensation



• $3 per hour based on experience and speed negotiable

• 40 hours per week with potential to manage additional stores

• Paid trial period to ensure a good fit

• Performance bonuses for accuracy and speed after probation



How to Apply ?



IMPORTANT: To show you've read this entire job posting and pay attention to detail which is critical for this role, include the mystery word in your application: #PRECISION



In your application include:



The mystery word #PRECISION or your application will not be reviewed



Your Shopify experience. How many products have you listed



Your typical listing speed in products per hour



Your availability. Timezone and hours per week



A short example of how you ensure accuracy in repetitive tasks

title: MEDICAL FRONT OFFICE ADMIN / SCHEDULER (Logistics Specialist) $7/hr + 13th MONTH
description:
Nearly Accentless English Required | Full-Time | 40 hours/week | 8:00 AM–5:00 PM EST

Location: Remote for US Eastern Time (Atlanta)



ABOUT US

Atlanta Lactation is a warm, all-female U.S. healthcare team working with moms and babies. We are a fully remote practice with 9 office locations, serving families across metro Atlanta and beyond through home visits, telehealth, and in-office appointments.



Our Team is Growing: We are adding an additional full-time, daytime admin position to complete our administrative core. Our Practice Owner, Katherine, is also currently working the front office. You will be joining a supportive, established environment where everyone knows both the "how" and the "why" of the work.



WHY THIS ROLE IS WORTH YOUR ATTENTION

 - Stability & Weekly Pay: Full-time, long-term role with 13th-month pay (pro-rated). We pay weekly via Wise.

 - Fee Coverage: We cover Wise/EasyPay fees so you receive your full rate.

 - Paid for Hours Worked: We value your time. Any extra time worked beyond your standard shift is fully paid.

 - No Scripts, No Robots: We don’t use scripts. You’ll be trained to deeply understand our business so you can speak naturally and confidently.

 - The "Puzzle" Factor: This is for the "logistics brain." If you love solving puzzles and watching a complex schedule click into place, the days will fly by.

 - Meaningful Work: You are helping families during one of the most vulnerable and beautiful times of their lives.

 - Innovation: The Practice Owner loves suggestions for improving workflows and patient experience. Her husband provides strong tech support to automate and optimize team requests.

 - Important Note: This is a hard role. It is complex, detail-heavy, and requires strong spatial reasoning. It is not a job where you just "fill slots." It requires a sharp mind that can visualize a map and juggle many moving parts.



CORE CHALLENGE

 - You will serve as "Mission Control" for our practice. This involves:

 - Geography: Routing home visit appointments across the massive Atlanta metropolitan area. You must be able to visualize a map in your mind, understanding where providers live, where they are starting their day, and how to move them through multiple locations without backtracking.

 - Logic: Managing bookings across 9 different office locations simultaneously.

 - Critical Thinking: Navigating an unusually complex insurance situation. You will have resources and training, but you must be able to speak to patients about their coverage with absolute confidence—without a script.

 - Triage: Handling queries about DME (breast pumps/supplies) and tagging the correct tea
mbers to ensure families get what they need.



CORE RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Logistics & Google Maps: Build efficient daily routes for multiple providers (IBCLCs). Compare drive times and geographic proximity to prevent backtracking.

 - Complex Scheduling (IntakeQ/PracticeQ): Manage home, office, and telehealth bookings according to insurance rules and provider availability.

 - Intelligent Communication (Spruce): Manage inbound calls and texts with "calm clarity." You must provide patients with exactly what they need to know without overwhelming them.

 - DME Coordination: Identify and tag the appropriate team for breast pump and supply queries.

 - Insurance Navigation: Explain copays, coverage status, and options to families clearly and professionally.

 - Internal Bridge: Communicate clearly with the billing team, providers, and the owner.



REQUIRED EXPERIENCE

 - You must meet at least ONE of the following, with a strong preference given to candidates who meet BOTH:

 - Required: Prior experience as a U.S. Medical VA (Front Office/Scheduling/EMR).

 - Required: Prior experience in Dispatch, Logistics, or Field-Service Scheduling. You must prove you have managed people "on the move."



TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS

 - Because this is a high-volume, real-time coordination role, your home office setup must be professional-grade and redundant.

 - Primary Internet: Fiber connection with a minimum of 50 Mbps download/upload speed.

 - Backup Internet (Required): A secondary ISP (different provider) or a high-speed mobile data hotspot to ensure zero downtime.

 - Power Redundancy: A reliable UPS or power station capable of keeping your router and laptop running during local power outages (brownouts).

 - Hardware: Laptop or Desktop with at least an i5 processor (or equivalent) and 8GB RAM (16GB preferred) to handle multitasking.

 - Noise-Canceling Headset: A high-quality, USB-connected headset with a noise-canceling microphone.

 - Workspace: A dedicated, quiet, and private workspace free from background noise.



SKILLS

 - Spatial Reasoning: You must be able to hold an image of a map in your mind and make logical routing decisions.

 - Native-Level English: High clarity and a nearly accentless voice.

 - No Script Dependency: You must be smart, curious, and able to think on your feet.

 - Overnight Experience: You have documented experience working from home during the US Eastern day (PH night).

 - Commitment: You can commit to working no other roles and will have no childcare or home duties during your shift.



TIME OFF & HOLIDAYS

 - Paid U.S. Holidays: Christmas Day, New Year’s Day, and U.S. Thanksgiving are paid days off. We typically work other U.S. holidays.

 - Flexibility: Unpaid time off is available for Philippines holidays, family events, or medical appointments with advance notice.

 - Support: We are an understanding and flexible team; your health and well-being matter to us!



COMPENSATION

 - Hourly Rate: Starting at up to $7.00 USD/hour, based on experience.

 - 13th Month Pay: Provided (pro-rated during the first year).

 - Growth: Pay reviewed after 90 days and annually (Pay ceiling for this role: $8.00/hr).



HOW TO APPLY (WE CAN’T WAIT TO HEAR FROM YOU!)

We want you to succeed! To help us get to know your unique strengths, please message us here on OJP. If you
 my practice directly, you will be disqualified. 



* The Subject Line of your message MUST be: "Atlanta Admin and Logistics Specialist - [Your Name]"



Include THREE THINGS as follows:



#1 Your application message 

In the body of your message, please address the following in detail:

 - Your Logistics Expertise: Summarize your experience managing routes, using Google Maps, or dispatching in a fast-paced environment.

 - Your Medical Background: Mention the specific EMRs you have used and your experience explaining U.S. insurance benefits to patients.

 - Your Problem-Solving Style: Since we do not use scripts, explain how you handle complex situations independently.

 - Your Commitment: Confirm the pay is acceptable, that you have availability for the 8:00 AM–5:00 PM EST shift beginning on whatever start date you would be available, and that you have no other work or home commitments during these hours.



#2 Your resume link. After the above message, add your direct resume link.



#3 Your Vocaroo or video link. After the resume link, include your recording link. Please BEGIN your recording by reading the scenario: "Hi, this is [Name] calling from Atlanta Lactation. I’m following up on your request for a home visit today. We were able to work you in at 2:00 PM! I’ve also double-checked your insurance. You’ll just have the $85 travel fee and no other fees. We can go ahead and get you booked if that works for you. Great. Happy to help with that. We do have a 48 hour cancellation and reschedule policy, so I just want to make sure you’re ready to go ahead? Any changes made to the appointment after booking would be subject to the $175 fee. Sounds good. Lets get you set up.”

Include in your video Your Story (After the script): Logistics/Mapping Experience: Tell us about a time you managed a complex schedule or routed people "on the move." Med VA Experience: Describe your role and experience explaining benefits to patients. Why Us? Where are you currently working, and why are you looking for a long-term (and script-free) role? Your Setup: A quick mention of your home office, noise-canceling headset, and experience staying tech-ready.



Note: Please be direct and professional. We value clear, concise, calm-yet-confident communication!

title: Executive Interview Lead for LinkedIn CEO Outreach
description:
We are publishing written CEO interviews and need someone to lead LinkedIn outreach to identify and secure senior executives aligned with our work across revenue management, cost structure, cash flow, risk, and strategic decision-making. You will identify relevant CEOs, conduct targeted outreach that positions the interview as a selective executive feature, and secure participation; once a CEO agrees, you will notify us, we will provide the Q&A, and you will manage communication, follow-ups, and responses through to a clean, publish-ready interview. Success is defined by your ability to consistently convert outreach into multiple high-quality CEO interview



Need to secure 5 interviews for $50, and you get paid an additional $10 per interview beyond that.



Candidate and Company Criteria

Focus on senior executives at established companies. We are not targeting early-stage founders or small businesses. We are not targeting US companies. Focus on Europe, Canada, Australia, and other established markets. Do not target emerging markets.



Outreach and Messaging

We provide the outreach message. You are responsible for executing it consistently and targeting the right profiles. You must provide the full 
munication history for every target, including your outreach and the executive’s responses, so we can retain it for records.



Process Flow



You identify and reach out to relevant executives using our message

You share the full LinkedIn message thread once there is a response

If they accept, you collect their
 address

You notify us and we take over the Q&A directly

Once the executive submits responses, the interview is considered complete



Success is defined by your ability to convert outreach into completed, publishable CEO interviews.
