description:
About Us:

We are an organic skincare brand focused on making science-backed skincare accessible and fun. We're inspired by viral Instagram accounts 
 which use cute, animated explanations to break down complex topics. If you love creating short-form videos that educate, entertain, and explode in views, we want you to help us replicate and innovate on that style!



See examples here: 



Important: Please only reply if you have experience making these types of videos. 



Job Description:

We're seeking a skilled video editor to produce high-engagement AI animated reels and shorts in the style 
 content. Their videos feature a friendly robot character (like "Ayubot") that simplifies skincare science through vibrant visuals, showing how ingredients interact with skin layers. These clips are cute, informative, and highly shareable, often going viral due to their relatable animations, quick pacing, and easy-to-understand breakdowns.

Your role will involve figuring out and mastering techniques to create similar videos—think layer-by-layer ingredient visuals, motion graphics, text overlays, and upbeat music that keeps viewers hooked. 



Key Responsibilities:

Design, generate and edit short-form AI Animated videos (15-60 seconds) that explain skincare concepts using animations, visual effects, and illustrative graphics including: 



Edit weekly short-form content that matches brief and style guidelines

Add captions + text animations that increase retention

Choose music, sound effects, and mix audio for impact

Maintain brand aesthetic while staying trend-forward

title: Public Records Data Researcher / Scraper
description:
???? HIRING: Public Records Data Researcher / Scraper (Remote | Project + Retainer)



We're looking for a sharp, resourceful data researcher who knows how to navigate government websites, county portals, and public records systems to find, document, and organize processes at scale.



This role has two main areas of responsibility:



???? PART 1: Real Estate Lead Source Research



You'll be responsible for researching and fully documenting the exact processes, online portals, submission
s, and contact information for sourcing distressed property leads across unlimited counties. This includes:



? County portals and processes for code violations

? Pre-probate and probate filing access

? Distressed property and vacant property lists

? Tax delinquent and lien records

? Any publicly available lead sources for real estate wholesaling



???? PART 2: Construction Permit & Inspection Research



Same concept, different data. You'll be finding and documenting public portals and processes for:



? Building permits (new builds, renovations)

? Electrical permits

? Strapping and insulation inspections

? General inspection schedules and records

? Any permit activity that signals active construction projects



???? WHAT THE DELIVERABLE ACTUALLY LOOKS LIKE:



This is not just a spreadsheet of URLs and
s. For every county and every data source, we need a full, detailed SOP that anyone on our team could follow without asking a single question. That means:



? If there's an online portal: exactly what to click, what menus to navigate, what dropdowns to select, screen by screen

? The exact filters to use to pull the data we need

? If there are patterns in permit numbers, violation numbers, or case IDs, document those patterns so we can search efficiently

? If it's a FOIA or public records submission: the exact
, what to write, what to reference, and what format the response comes back in

? Any quirks, workarounds, or tricks you find along the way that make the process faster or more reliable

? Everything organized so we can scale this to any new county and hit the ground running



You're building the playbook. The standard is that someone with zero experience in that county's system could sit down, follow your documentation, and pull the data correctly on the first try.



???? COMPENSATION STRUCTURE:



This is a two-phase role:



1. Paid Project: Initial buildout of the full research database and process documentation. Scope and pay will be discussed based on experience and speed.

2. Monthly Retainer: Once the system is built, you'll be kept on retainer for ongoing maintenance, adding new counties, updating processes when portals change, and expanding coverage as needed.



We're not looking for someone to just copy and paste URLs. We need someone who can dig, problem-solve when a county doesn't make it easy, and deliver clean, usable documentation every time.



Requirements:

? Experience navigating government and public records websites

? Strong attention to detail and documentation skills

? Ability to work independently and figure things out without hand-holding

? Comfortable working across dozens of counties and varying portal formats

? Clear communication in English



???? HOW TO APPLY:



In your application, do NOT just send a generic cover letter. We want to see how you think. Write out your initial thought process for how you would approach this work. What steps would you take? How would you go about finding these portals, documenting the processes, and organizing the data? Walk us through your methodology.



That's what we're evaluating. If you can't clearly explain how you'd tackle this, you're not the right fit.

title: Capcut Video Editor
description:
Looking for full time video editor for a short form social media type of videos.



You will be editing and making AI videos using AI tools like Veo3, Gemini, Higgsfield, Heygen, and ElevenLabs. You will be generating video clips / AI characters talking and editing them with Capcut.



You will be trained extensively on how to do it, previous experience using CapCut is required but the other tools you don't need any experience in. It'll be mostly just providing scripts to an AI tool and then adding the voice to a capcut video. It's quite easy once you get the hang of it, which we'll train you to do!



Requirements:



- Attention to detail

- STRONG English

- Strong internet connection

- Previous experience with Capcut / TikTok / Instagram

- Good working with a team

- Ability to learn and take constructive feedback

- Availability for 6-8h a day (never more, sometimes less)
